interpretation: Revenue compounded strongly over the decade.

Operating cash flow grew faster than revenue.

formula: reported OCF; TTM derived from FY minus Q1 plus Q1
interpretation: Cash generation available before capex.

formula: reported purchases of property and equipment; TTM derived
interpretation: PPE purchases are the cash capex denominator.
FCF is not a 10-K line; it is the cash left after PPE purchases.
Higher values mean more operating cash is consumed by infrastructure.
This is the visible conversion pressure from capex.

formula: cash-flow add-back: depreciation of property and equipment
interpretation: Observable floor/proxy for replacement burden; not the same as maintenance capex.
Rough growth/expansion pipeline proxy, but not proof of growth capex.

formula: 10-K/10-Q PPE note; blank if not pulled comparably
